mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and economic growth expectations, which increase inflation expectations and real yields, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363696M as of April 2014 reflects post-recession recovery momentum
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS growth fails to correlate with 20-day Treasury yield changes (Spearman rank correlation < 0.15 over any 12-month rolling window), or yield falls despite RSAFS increases in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor market and rising wage pressure, which increases inflation expectations and causes the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the Fed is expected to tighten policy.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% or declines by more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.5% in April 2014, down from 7.0% one year prior
failure_condition: UNRATE declines below 6.0% but 60-day Treasury yield does not rise, or yield falls in > 55% of instances when UNRATE drops by 0.3pp+ over 3-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows persistent momentum, creating negative real rates that compress Treasury yields downward as investors demand compensation for inflation erosion, causing the 10-year yield to fall.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate is negative (real rates < 0%)
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% and CPIAUCSL at 218.813 as of May 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: Real federal funds rate remains negative but 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 20-day forward windows, or the interaction effect (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above trend, signaling supply-side expansion and productivity gains that reduce inflation pressure and allow the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% or 3-month growth rate accelerates above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.254 (2017=100) in March 2014, reflecting post-recession manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: INDPRO growth accelerates above 1.5% over 3 months but 5-day Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances, or Spearman correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day yield changes is positive and > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal strong demand momentum that raises growth expectations and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900K annualized units AND RSAFS shows month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 817K and RSAFS at 363696M in March-April 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS growth signals trigger simultaneously but 20-day Treasury yield falls in > 50% of instances, or the joint interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) produces rank IC < 0.08 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K per month, indicating robust labor demand that raises inflation expectations and signals the Fed will begin tightening, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average monthly payroll growth exceeds 200K or month-over-month growth > 250K
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136899K in April 2014, reflecting consistent post-recession job creation
regime_hint: accelerating labor market recovery approaching full employment
failure_condition: 3-month average PAYEMS growth exceeds 200K but 60-day Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day yield changes is negative and < -0.12.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels combined with real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized creates an unsustainably loose policy stance that markets expect will normalize, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors anticipate future Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5% while annualized GDPC1 growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% and GDPC1 at 11720B (2017 dollars) in Q1 2014
regime_hint: policy normalization cycle with accelerating growth
failure_condition: Policy accommodation (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%) persists with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% but 60-day Treasury yield falls in > 60% of instances, or the interaction effect shows rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year while industrial production (INDPRO) grows steadily, indicating a disinflationary supply-driven expansion that allows the Fed to maintain low rates, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as inflation expectations remain anchored.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPI inflation below 2.0% AND INDPRO 3-month growth rate positive
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.813 and INDPRO at 107.254 in March 2014, consistent with low inflation environment
regime_hint: disinflationary supply expansion with anchored expectations
failure_condition: CPI remains below 2% with positive INDPRO growth but 20-day Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × INDPRO) produces rank IC < 0.07 over any 12-month rolling window.
